item.title: Watch: Van Hollen and Blanche get into heated exchange over "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: Democratic Sen. Chris Van Hollen and acting Attorney General Todd Blanche got into a heated exchange during a Senate hearing on Tuesday. Van Hollen questioned Blanche about the creation of the $1.7+ billion "anti-weaponization fund" and the Jeffrey Epstein files.

item.title: American doctor working in Congo tests positive for Ebola
item.description: An American medical missionary in the Democratic Republic of the Congo was evacuated after testing positive for Ebola.

item.title: Trump says he was "an hour away" from striking Iran
item.description: Trump says he scrapped a planned attack on Iran at the request of Gulf allies as "serious negotiations" on a peace deal are underway.

item.title: Can Social Security survivor benefits be garnished for debt?
item.description: Debt collectors can be aggressive, but Social Security survivor benefits may be more protected than you'd expect.

item.title: Have a CD account set to mature this June? Here's what to do next.
item.description: Is your CD account maturity date on the calendar this June? Here are three things to consider doing right now.

item.title: Missionary leader shares update on American doctor who contracted Ebola in Congo
item.description: Matt Allison, the executive director of the missionary group Serge, joins CBS News with more details on the American doctor who contracted Ebola while working in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.title: U.S. slaps top Cuban officials with new sanctions
item.description: The U.S. announced its latest round of sanctions against the Cuban government and military officials on Monday as tensions ramp up in the region. CBS News' Cristian Benavides has more.

item.title: What we know about the American with Ebola being treated in Germany
item.description: Dr. Peter Stafford, a Serge medical missionary serving in Congo, is being treated in Germany after testing positive for Ebola. CBS News' Celine Gounder has more details.

item.title: Chinese companies probed over cuts to shipping container production before pandemic
item.description: Investigators have been looking at a handful of Chinese firms that together control the majority of unrefrigerated shipping container manufacturing around the globe, the sources said.

item.title: Putin visiting China following historic Trump-Xi summit
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is visiting China a week after President Trump's summit with Xi Jinping. CBS News' Holly Williams reports.

item.title: Why Trump says he called off a scheduled attack on Iran
item.description: President Trump says some American allies in the Middle East reached out as the U.S. prepared a scheduled attack on Iran. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi reports.

item.title: Key primary races to watch as Trump endorses preferred candidates
item.description: Several states are holding primaries on Tuesday, and President Trump is endorsing some candidates. CBS News' Zak Hudak and Taurean Small report.

item.title: Trump gives tour of White House ballroom construction, says he was close to resuming Iran attacks
item.description: President Trump spoke with reporters on Tuesday as he toured the construction site for the White House ballroom. Mr. Trump also commented on the apparent plans to resume strikes against Iran before he announced progress in talks.

item.title: Security guard killed in San Diego Islamic Center shooting remembered as a hero
item.description: A security guard who was among the victims killed in a shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego is being hailed for his heroism and bravery during the attack.

item.title: Georgia voters head to polls for major primaries and key local races
item.description: Georgia voters are heading to the polls on Tuesday to decide on candidates for the U.S. Senate, governor and lieutenant governor. Some key local races for agricultural commissioner and state Supreme Court are also in play. CBS News' Skyler Henry has more.

item.title: Student describes hearing gunshots at Islamic Center of San Diego during shooting
item.description: Video shows children fleeing the scene of a deadly shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego on Monday. CBS News' Anna Schecter has the latest details.

item.title: Live chicks hatched from artificial eggshell, biotech company says
item.description: Independent scientists say the technology, while impressive, lacks some components to be truly considered an artificial egg.

item.title: Who's who in Cuba's leadership? Here are the names to know.
item.description: President Trump has been openly mulling a takeover of Cuba similar to the operation that toppled Venezuela's Nicolás Madoro. What it would mean for the regime's leaders remains to be seen. Here are some of the figures to watch.

item.title: These everyday habits can damage your skin barrier
item.description: Americans spend billions every year on skin care products, but experts say using too many products could hurt your skin. Dr. Rachel Nazarian explains how to protect what's known as your skin barrier and why it's important.

item.title: Sustainability-focused clothing brand to reportedly be bought by Shein
item.description: U.S. clothing brand Everlane is reportedly being sold to the Chinese company Shein, which has faced controversy over its business practices. Puck Fashion correspondent Lauren Sherman, who first reported the deal, discusses what to know and the potential impact on consumers.

item.title: Beanie Feldstein on becoming a parent and what she wants readers to take from kids' book
item.description: Actor Beanie Feldstein talks about the theme of her debut children's book, which focuses on the power of friendship. She also discusses becoming a parent soon and starring in a new film with Robert De Niro.

item.title: What are today's mortgage interest rates: May 19, 2026?
item.description: Want to buy a home or refinance your current one? Here are the mortgage interest rates you'll need to know now.

item.title: Iran stages mass weddings for couples ready for "self-sacrifice" in war
item.description: Iranian state media showed wedding ceremonies in Tehran for dozens of couples said to have volunteered for "self-sacrifice" in the war with the U.S.

item.title: Ebola strain in latest outbreak has no vaccine, no treatment
item.description: The virus behind the latest Ebola outbreak is the Bundibugyo virus, which is less common and there is no vaccine or treatment.

item.title: Troops say Army ignored request for more medical support before deadly attack
item.description: Survivors say they'd asked for more medical support before the Iranian drone strike that killed six U.S. soldiers at their command post in Kuwait in the war's first 24 hours.

item.title: Eroding ACA enrollment portends higher insurance rates
item.description: An uptick in people skipping Obamacare premium payments in many states suggests the Affordable Care Act's rising costs are hitting home for 2026 enrollees.

item.title: The story of Cuba's 1996 shootdown that could lead to Raúl Castro's indictment
item.description: Thirty years ago, a Cuban fighter jet shot down two civilian planes operated by Florida-based exile group Brothers to the Rescue, an incident that inflamed U.S.-Cuba relations.

item.title: Trump admin. proposes admitting thousands more Afrikaners to U.S. as refugees
item.description: The Trump administration is doubling down on efforts to resettle White Afrikaners from South Africa as refugees, proposing to increase the government's refugee cap to welcome thousands more of them, according to a plan obtained by CBS News.

item.title: Pay for American workers is lagging inflation — again
item.description: U.S. wage growth is lagging inflation for the first time since 2023, eroding consumers' purchasing power.

item.title: Why "The Odyssey" was shot entirely on IMAX film
item.description: Correspondent Scott Pelley and director Christopher Nolan visited FotoKem, the last motion picture lab in the world that makes 70mm prints, to see finishing touches being made to "The Odyssey," the first feature shot entirely on IMAX film.
